results.possibleIdentity: Ronald W. Harrison was a computer programmer and engineer specializing in optics and synthetic holography at International Business Machines (IBM) in the late 1960s. He was based at IBM's research and manufacturing facility located at Monterey and Cottle Roads in San Jose, California. His technical expertise included computer programming, optics, lasers, and the study of diffraction patterns. Harrison was a registered technical member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded in 1966 to foster collaborations between the scientific and artistic communities. Within the E.A.T. network, he offered his skills as a technical consultant to artists, particularly in the emerging field of computer-generated (synthetic) holography, which was a significant area of research at the IBM San Jose laboratory during that period.
